column8: Did you know that there are people___________are paid to predict the future?
column9: They're called actuaries. I'd never heard actuaries until my friend Greg____________mother is an actuary, told me about them.
column11: Actuaries usually work for companies, like insurance companies,____________deal with the chances of things happening in the future.
column12: For example, it's more probable that buildings will be flooded in places___________it rains a lot.
column13: Greg's mum,_______________has been actuary for about five years, is involved with car insurance.
column14: She works in an office_____________they decide how much car insurance people should pay.
column15: It's more probable that a car_________is new is safer and more reliable than a very old car, so people_____________cars are new pay less insurance.
column16: If there are drivers______________she thinks will probably have more accidents, she makes them pay more insurance!
